description: Fires a tag when a visitor is a member of any or a specific audience (Data Activation). Membership is checked at the beginning of the session and periodically during the session. Combine with conditions such as Page URL, Page path, referrer, campaign, or traffic source to fire only when an audience member visits specific pages or meets other criteria. Available from 17.0.0; requires Customer data platform and Data Activation in the Piwik PRO suite.
api_note: In the API this trigger uses trigger_type 'cdp_audience_detection'. The product name in the Piwik PRO suite is Data Activation (formerly CDP).

event_condition.description: Fires the tag when audience data has been fetched. The trigger must include a condition that the Event variable equals stg.audiencesFetched.

builtin_variable.name: Event
builtin_variable.description: A variable used in the CDP audience detection trigger. It identifies the event that indicates audiences have been fetched.
builtin_variable.usage: Use this variable_id in the condition that defines when the trigger fires. Set the condition value to "stg.audiencesFetched" and condition_type (e.g. equals).

cdp_audience_detection_conditions_refere.note: The audience detection condition must use the built-in variable 'Event' (id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6). Set value to "stg.audiencesFetched". Add other conditions (audience, Page URL, referrer, etc.) as needed.

condition_types.description: Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and visits a page with the selected page URL
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and visits a page with the selected page path
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and visits a page on the selected hostname
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and a history event occurs (pushState, replaceState, popState or hashchange)
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and the URL hash matches the selected condition
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and the previous URL hash matches
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and the history state matches
condition_types.example: History state is set or History state equals my-state

condition_types.description: Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and the previous history state matches
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and came from the selected internal referrer
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and came from the selected external referrer
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and came from the selected campaign (utm_campaign)
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and traffic source matches (campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct)
Fires when the visitor is a member of the audience and is a returning visitor (stg_returning_visitor cookie)
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Show different content or fire tags only for visitors in a Data Activation audience
Personalize or target tags (e.g. remarketing, offers) based on audience membership
Fire tags when an audience member visits specific pages or meets page/referrer conditions
Combine audience membership with page URL, path, referrer, or campaign for precise triggers
common_use_cases: Fire a tag when Apple users audience visits the product-tour page
Fire a tag when high-value segment visits pricing or checkout pages
Trigger on audience members coming from a specific campaign or referrer
Run tags only for returning visitors who belong to an audience
best_practices: Use Data Activation audiences defined in Piwik PRO; membership is evaluated at session start and periodically
Add page or referrer conditions to avoid firing on every page for large audiences
Use descriptive trigger names that include the audience and the condition (e.g. High-value – Product page)

create_trigger.description: Create an Audience detection (Data Activation) trigger. Use when the visitor must be a member of an audience. Set trigger_type to 'cdp_audience_detection' (API value).

trigger_type.description: Must be 'cdp_audience_detection' for Data Activation audience detection triggers (API)

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include one condition with variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 (Event), value "stg.audiencesFetched" (e.g. condition_type equals). May also include audience selection, Page URL, Page path, referrer, campaign, etc.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing Audience detection (Data Activation) trigger – only editable fields will be processed

name.description: Human-readable trigger name that describes the audience and/or context

trigger_type.description: Trigger type (API: cdp_audience_detection). Product name: Data Activation.

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include one condition with the built-in Event variable (variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6), value "stg.audiencesFetched", and condition_type (e.g. equals). May also include audience, Page URL, Page path, Referrer, Campaign, etc.

structure.variable_id: For the required event condition use 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 (Event); for others use the appropriate variable UUID
structure.condition_type: equals (for Event = stg.audiencesFetched) or other comparison
structure.value: For the Event condition use "stg.audiencesFetched"; for others use the appropriate value

conditions.recommendation: Include one condition with variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6, condition_type equals, value "stg.audiencesFetched"; add audience/page/referrer conditions as needed.

audience_product_page_example.description: Fire when a Data Activation audience member visits the product-tour page

audience_page_path_example.description: Fire when an audience member visits a page whose path contains /product-tour

parameters.name: Data Activation audience – Product tour path

common_mistakes: Omitting the Event condition (variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 with value stg.audiencesFetched)
Using this trigger without Data Activation / Customer data platform enabled in the app
Expecting immediate audience updates – membership is checked at session start and periodically
Creating triggers that fire on every page for large audiences without adding page or referrer conditions

possible_causes: Missing Event condition (must use variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6, value stg.audiencesFetched)
Visitor is not in the selected Data Activation audience
Data Activation or audience sync not enabled for the site
debugging_steps: Ensure one condition has variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 (Event), condition_type equals, value "stg.audiencesFetched"
Verify the visitor is in the audience in Data Activation
Check that conditions (e.g. Page URL, path) match the current page
Confirm trigger_type is cdp_audience_detection in the API
